commit_scope: Add browser-local Field Model Form forward/reverse runtime proof while keeping Go/Rust claims bounded to structural constructors.

commands: cd web && npm test -- form-kernel-client.test.ts form-kernel-field-runtime.test.ts
local_validation.notes: Browser TypeScript now has executable FMF fieldStep, intervention, reverseReceipt, lift/project, conflict residual, and unit-carrying field-law tests. The shared cross-kernel Form proof still returns 93.

ci_validation.notes: Pending push, PR, and GitHub checks.

e2e_validation.expected_behavior_delta: The public web kernel bundle has a browser-local FMF runtime available for tested forward/reverse field execution, beyond constructor-only FMF primitives.
test_flows: cd web && npm test -- form-kernel-client.test.ts form-kernel-field-runtime.test.ts

phase_gate.reason: Browser forward/reverse FMF execution is proven; Go and Rust still need native runtime value layers before claiming generic fieldStep execution.

evidence_refs: web/lib/form-kernel/field-runtime.ts binds FMF execution primitives to the browser-vendored Form kernel.
web/tests/form-kernel-field-runtime.test.ts proves DNA sequence lift/project, forward rule execution, observer intervention, reverse receipt, conflict residuals, and Ohm-law field execution.
specs/field-model-form-v0.md and docs/coherence-substrate/field-model-form.form mark browser TypeScript forward/reverse runtime as validated while preserving the Go/Rust gap.
scripts/validate_commit_evidence.py now treats web/lib runtime modules as runtime evidence, covered by api/tests/test_commit_evidence_validator.py.
